title: [FIRRTL] Reset inference don't work correctly
body:
My top module is `MambaAcc_CDC_syn` and the compiled firrtl of top module is

```
module MambaAcc_CDC_syn : @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 9:7]
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : AsyncReset
    output io : { scl : Analog<1>, sda : Analog<1>, ref_clk : Analog<1>, reset : Analog<1>, bypass : Analog<1>, lock : Analog<1>, gpio : Analog<18>, rx_clk : Analog<1>, rx_winc : Analog<1>, rx_wdata : Analog<33>, rx_wfull : Analog<1>, tx_clk : Analog<1>, tx_rinc : Analog<1>, tx_rdata : Analog<32>, tx_rempty : Analog<1>} @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 13:14]

    inst pll of PLLWrapper @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 39:19]
    connect pll.clock, clock
    connect pll.reset, reset
...
```

The output error is

```
ExitCode:
1
STDOUT:

STDERR:
<stdin>:343513:11: error: reset network "reset" simultaneously connected to async and sync resets
    input reset : AsyncReset
          ^
<stdin>:343513:11: note: majority of connections to this reset are sync
<stdin>:343518:5: note: async drive here:
    connect pll.reset, reset
    ^
```

I'm confused about the top module reset has sync driver leading to `note: majority of connections to this reset are sync` output.

FIRRTL version 3.3.0
code_blocks:
module MambaAcc_CDC_syn : @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 9:7]
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : AsyncReset
    output io : { scl : Analog<1>, sda : Analog<1>, ref_clk : Analog<1>, reset : Analog<1>, bypass : Analog<1>, lock : Analog<1>, gpio : Analog<18>, rx_clk : Analog<1>, rx_winc : Analog<1>, rx_wdata : Analog<33>, rx_wfull : Analog<1>, tx_clk : Analog<1>, tx_rinc : Analog<1>, tx_rdata : Analog<32>, tx_rempty : Analog<1>} @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 13:14]

    inst pll of PLLWrapper @[src/main/scala/MambaAcc_CDC_syn.scala 39:19]
    connect pll.clock, clock
    connect pll.reset, reset
...
ExitCode:
1
STDOUT:

STDERR:
<stdin>:343513:11: error: reset network "reset" simultaneously connected to async and sync resets
    input reset : AsyncReset
          ^
<stdin>:343513:11: note: majority of connections to this reset are sync
<stdin>:343518:5: note: async drive here:
    connect pll.reset, reset
    ^
